key_evidence: Oracle is finalizing 30,000 layoffs (~18% of global workforce) by June 15, 2026.
Oracle counted the 60-day WARN notice period as part of severance rather than paying it on top.
Workers faced a 'sign release or forfeit severance' ultimatum; unvested retention shares were forfeited.
Leadership framed the strategy as 'choosing the chips' — explicit prioritization of margin over workforce.

evidence.quote: employees must sign a release waiving their right to sue in order to receive any benefit at all.
evidence.claim: Oracle conditioned all severance on signing a legal release (coercive consent)

evidence.quote: Oracle included the 60-day WARN notice pay within its existing severance calculation rather than paying it on top of severance.
evidence.claim: Oracle counted WARN notice pay inside severance rather than in addition

evidence.quote: We are choosing the chips. Anyone whose job is not making the chips run faster for our customers is at risk in this industry.
evidence.claim: Leadership explicitly framed strategy as prioritizing chips/AI over workforce
